title: Keybinds & Shortcuts
summary: A quick control sheet for the buttons players use most often.
intro: A simple control sheet for the buttons that matter most out in the field. Made to be checked fast and closed just as fast.

shortcutGroups.title: Essential Shortcuts
shortcutGroups.description: The buttons players will reach for constantly during normal play.

entries.description: Standard movement keys for exploration, positioning, and combat.
Hold to move faster across open ground.
Use for jumps, ledges, and low obstacles.
Talk to characters, pick things up, and use doors or camp stations.
Check gear, quick slots, and important items.
Open the current map and your active objective view.
Use the tool or context action you have equipped.
Open the pause menu or back out of the screen you are in.

shortcutGroups.title: Combat & Field Actions
shortcutGroups.description: Less frequent, but still worth finding fast when things get messy.

entries.description: Use your main attack, tool, or equipped action.
Use an alternate fire mode, aim state, or secondary tool.
Cycle quick-slot tools without opening the inventory.
Toggle the support action used for visibility or clue tracking.

sections.heading: Using This Page
paragraphs: This page is here for speed. You should be able to glance at it once and get straight back to playing.
If a key matters during normal play, it belongs near the top. If it only matters once in a while, it can sit lower down.
items: Keep movement and interaction together so the core loop is easy to scan.
Only add extra description when an action is easy to forget under pressure.
If controller support gets added later, mirror this layout instead of mixing formats.
